observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the inventory committee to address the noted discrepancies in the
RPCPPE to come up with accurate and more reliable reports; and
b. Direct the Inventory Committee, together with the MGSO and Municipal
Accounting Office, to revisit the COA Circular No. 2020-006 to complete the
procedural guidelines for one-time cleansing of PPE balances, and to eliminate
the noted discrepancy amounting to P184,357,738.83 as of December 31, 2024.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Designated Municipal
Accountant to coordinate with the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Officer (LDRRMO) in identifying and addressing the cause/s of the
discrepancy between the unutilized balance per Utilization Report and the amount
reported in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Designated Municipal
Accountant to ensure that the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF is transferred
to the STF at year-end under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund
books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Designated Municipal
Accountant to provide sufficient disclosure of the LDRRMF in the Notes to the
Financial Statements as required under Section 5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Designated Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to determine the origin and details of the
reported discrepancy between the reciprocal accounts Due to and Due from Other
Funds.

Moreover, instruct the Designated Municipal Accountant to provide schedules
and necessary disclosures of the aforementioned accounts in the Notes to
Financial Statements, consistent with section 111 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of
the year and submit it to the Designated Municipal Accountant; and
b. Designated Municipal Accountant, based on the report from the Municipal
Treasurer, to prepare the JEV for the restoration of cash equivalent of the
unreleased checks and recognize the appropriate payable/liability accounts
and in addition, reverse the entries made in the ensuing year, in compliance
with the pertinent provisions of COA GAFMIS Circular Letter No. 2002-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Designated Municipal
Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official or
employee without the liquidation of prior cash advances.

In addition, consider designating additional Disbursing Officers to facilitate cash
disbursement activities efficiently.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Designated Municipal
Accountant to limit the grant of cash advances to the Accountable Officer
within the allowable maximum cash accountability covered by the bond,
according to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Treasury Circular 02-2009.
Otherwise, authorize the Accountable Officer to apply for an increase in the
coverage of her bond to commensurate her actual cash accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Designated Municipal
Accountant to maintain the subsidiary ledgers for the unexpended balances of
LDDRMF by year of transfer to facilitate the monitoring of the five (5) year period
in which the funds shall remain in the STF and the reversion of any remaining
balance to the General Fund after said period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Designated Municipal
Accountant to cause the immediate submission of a list of recipients, their
respective addresses and the items given to them, among other information
necessary to facilitate validation by the Audit Team and establish the propriety
and validity of such transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Designated Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Stop the practice of processing payments without complete supporting
documents; and
b. Coordinate with responsible officials to facilitate the immediate submission to
the Audit Team of the lacking documents for verification and audit.

i. Posting requirement of the Updated Annual Procurement Plan (APP) which serves as basis in the procurement of goods and services; as well as Notice of Award (NOA) together with the approved contracts or Purchase Orders (POs) in the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) online portal and in the Municipality’s website were not complied with, contrary to provided services as required under Section 4, Item (u) RA No. 11494 and Section 3.2 of Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) Circular No. 01-2020.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Secretariat to post the updated Annual Procurement Plan, Notice of Award (NOA) together with the approved contracts or Purchase Orders (POs) in the Municipality’s Government’s PhilGEPS account, as well as, Municipality’s bulletin board.
ii. Procurements of goods in response to affected communities due to COVID-19 pandemic totaling P15,784,623.86; and 5% LDRRMF amounting to P15,784,623.86 were not supported with complete documentation, contrary to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-01, thereby precluded the timely auditorial review and evaluation. Also, Delivery Reports were not furnished within 24 hours after acceptance of deliveries, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-002. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC, Municipal Accountant to:
a) submit immediately all the lacking supporting documents for COVID-related disbursements amounting to P15,784,623.86; and
b) ensure that the required documents are complete before payments are made in accordance with the provisions of the GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and Joint Memorandum (JMC) No. 2020-01 to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction:
No action was taken by the management.
The current OIC-Municipal Accountant assumed only in June 2023.
There was no proper turn-over of documents by the outgoing Municipal Accountant. Thus, some documents such as supporting documents for COVID-related procurements were not submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The current OIC-Municipal Accountant assumed only in June 2023. There was no proper turn-over of documents by the outgoing Municipal Accountant. Thus, some documents such as supporting documents for COVID-related procurements were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Office and the Municipal General Services Officer to furnish copies of delivery documents to the Auditor within 24 hours after acceptance of deliveries, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-002.
observations.managementAction: Submission of POs and Delivery Receipts are already posted in the Municipality’s PhilGEPS account, as well as, Municipality’s bulletin board.
observations.reason: Perfected infrastructure contracts were not submitted to the Audit Team. Thus, some supporting documents for the said transactions were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC, Municipal Accountant to continue updating and completing their Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELCs) and Property Cards (PCs) and reconcile it with the inventory report and property records; and the Designated MGSO to continue updating and completing their records with subsidiary ledgers of the Acting Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The OIC – Municipal Accountant and Designated GSO are on the process of implementation of the recommended undertaking.
observations.reason: Updating of records and inadequate manpower hindered the early completion of the required undertakings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC, Municipal Accountant to continue updating and completing their records with subsidiary ledgers and reconcile their records; and the Designated MGSO to prepare and submit the following: • the Report on the Local Road Network (RLRN) in the format prescribed in Annex A of the COA Circular No. 2015-008; • the Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and • the Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) in the format prescribed in Annex E of the Circular and the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System (RPCRNS) in the format prescribed in Annex C of the Circular.
observations.managementAction: On-going implementation of the audit recommendation. The Municipality has already designated a GSO who will be responsible of the reports. In addition, the Municipality has designated a GSO who will be responsible of the reports.
observations.reason: Inadequate manpower, as well as, incomplete record by the Municipal GSO hindered the early completion of the required undertakings for Road Network account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the municipal personnel in-charge to facilitate the transfer of titles of the acquired lots to the name of the Municipality. Also, MGSO and Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to obtain legal documents such as Deed of Donation, Deed of Absolute Sale needed for the transfer of title in the name of the Municipality. It is likewise recommended that properties with absolute ownership by the Municipality be subjected to appraisal necessary for recording purpose.
observations.managementAction: Utmost effort to locate the missing legal documents was undertaken. Identified titles were transferred in the name of the Municipality. However, some legal documents needed to facilitate in the transfer of title such as Deed of Donation and Deed of Absolute Sale cannot be located.
observations.reason: Inadequate record and lack of proper turn-over of legal documents hindered the transfer of titles. Thus, some required documents such as Deed of Donation, Deed of Absolute Sale cannot be located.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC, Municipal Accountant make an account analysis to support adjustments in the Land account and draw Journal Entry Voucher, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The OIC, Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted a certified list showing the account balances and undertook an account analysis.
observations.reason: Inadequate maintenance of detailed subsidiary record by the Municipal General Services Office hindered the identification of the individual Land item.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC, Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) and the Bids and Awards Committee to address the causes of non-submission of Contract and POs to the Office of the Auditor within five working days after its perfection/perfection of contract, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001. Likewise, furnish the Auditor with a copy of delivery documents as required under COA Circular No. 2009-002 to facilitate the timely conduct of inspection of the deliveries. Henceforth, comply strictly COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2009-002.
observations.managementAction: Advance copies of delivery documents (Notice of Awards and Purchase Orders) together with perfected contracts are posted in PhilGEPS and copies were forwarded to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Advance copies of perfected contracts were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee (IC) to complete the reconciliation of RPCPPE and PPE account balance to identify the cause of the variance; and the OIC, Municipal Accountant to maintain updated PPELC and Property Cards and utilize the result of the reconciliation in the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE. Henceforth, conduct a complete cycle of physical count of PPE items owned by the Municipal Government of Famy in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 80-124 dated January 18, 1980 and Manual on Property Custodianship.
observations.managementAction: On-going implementation of the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Non-maintenance of detailed subsidiary record by the Accounting Section, as well as, inadequate manpower, hindered the conduct of one-time cleansing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC, Municipal Accountant to maintain adequate record in the NTFS which will assist the users in understanding the accounts presented in the financial statements. Also, request the Municipal Engineering Office to provide the necessary information for the NTFS on Road Network.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2023 Notes to FS, the OIC-Municipal Accountant disclosed the Surplus/Deficit. Required information for Road Network was requested from the Municipal Engineering Office.
observations.reason: Information on Road Network were not provided by the Municipal Engineering Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to: a) direct the concerned persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances or instruct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the payment of the salaries or any amount due to the above named persons liable for the settlement of the amount disallowed/suspended; b) direct the concerned persons responsible for the settlement of the Notices of Suspension (NS); and c) submit to the Office of the Auditor copies of the Official Receipt issued by the Municipal Treasurer and/or other documents as proof of settlement of the amount disallowed/suspended.
observations.managementAction: Submitted proof for the settlement of two Notices. The Municipal management is coordinating for settlement of the remaining disallowed/suspended amount.
observations.reason: Submission of lacking documents for the settlement is still ongoing. Some persons liable for the disallowances have yet to settle their liabilities. Lack of compliance on submission of required documents impeded full implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC, Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official, officer or employee without liquidating first his/her prior cash advances. It is likewise recommended that the Municipal management to designate additional disbursing officer to limit cash advance handled by each DO/AO. Henceforth, comply strictly with Item 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The management stopped granting cash advances to elected officials since February 2023. However, multiple cash advances were still granted to disbursing officers without prior liquidation during CY 2023.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report, Audit Observation No. 10.1, there is lack of monitoring and control which contributed to the continuing instances of granting additional cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC, Municipal Accountant to: a) grant cash advance for salaries, wages, allowances, honoraria and other similar payments equal to the net amount of the payroll for a pay period; and b) subject the prepared payroll to review prior to its approval to ascertain the correctness of the same.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the management. However during the exit conference, the Municipal Mayor instructed the HRMO to prepare payrolls on the basis of valid documents, to be verified by the OIC-Municipal Accountant in order prepare accurate payroll and prevent excessive cash advance.
observations.reason: Inadequate review of supporting documents and inadequate control in payroll preparation; also some payrolls included personnel who are already separated or on leave without pay which were not properly excluded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to require the accountable officers to liquidate cash advances within the allowable period prescribed by COA Circular No. 97-002, otherwise, impose penalty which may include withholding salary. Henceforth, comply strictly with Items 4.1.3 and 5 of COA Circular 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the OIC-Municipal Accountant to send reminder letters to concerned personnel. Some liquidations were made.
observations.reason: Implementation of the audit recommendation is ongoing; proper turn-over of documents from outgoing officials caused delays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC, Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials other than their official travelling expenses and strictly comply to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, as well as, observance with sound internal control.
observations.managementAction: The management assured that no additional cash advances were granted to elected officials since February 2023, except advances for travels.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report, Audit Observation No. 10.3, some practices persisted before February 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to prepare documents necessary for disposal such as (a) properly filled-up Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), (b) property ledger card duly certified by the OIC-Municipal Accountant and submit to the Audit Team for inspection and review all the appraised value. It is likewise recommended that the MGSO conduct a periodic review of PPE records so as to establish the necessity of disposal. Henceforth, conduct the immediate disposal of unserviceable property and instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to derecognize in the books the PPE after disposal.
observations.managementAction: The designated MGSO acknowledged the observation and promised to implement the audit recommendations. Reports for the disposal will be prepared and the management will pursue for disposal once the relevant documents are available.
observations.reason: Reports and supporting documents for disposal are not yet completed; lack of proper documentation delayed disposal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to: a) coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and other Offices involved to the implementation of the planned priority projects in the AIP so that the constituents and general public could avail of the benefits therefrom at the earliest possible time; b) appropriate and utilize the 20 percent DF for PPAs that contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in accordance with DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and c) stop the practice of charging regular expenditures against the 20 percent Development Fund, otherwise the transactions shall be suspended/disallowed in audit.
It is also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct both the OIC, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile their reported accomplishments pertaining to 20 percent LDF account and immediately effect the necessary adjustments, if there’s any. Henceforth, strictly comply with the guidelines of the Joint Memorandum Circular of the DILG and DBM relative to the utilization of the 20 percent IRA for DFs so that only legitimate ones are charged against the fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator admitted the observations and assured to update its LDIP and subject the PPAs listed therein to screening, evaluation and ranking in order to propose feasible PPAs in the AIP.
observations.reason: No reason was provided; implementation is ongoing and some actions pending such as selection and screening of PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Human Resource Officer to: a) review and study the current plantilla position to come up with a more realistic organizational structure for the Municipality; b) prioritize filling-up the vacant positions either by hiring new employees or expediting promotion of deserving employees; c) limit the hiring of casuals and JOs to those that are extremely necessary according to the immediate and actual needs of each Office, with consideration on the financial capability of the Municipality; and d) fill-up the vacant plantilla position to hire qualified employees who will provide the regular services provided by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: On-going review of the plantilla. As of December 31, 2023, there were 13 unfilled plantilla positions.
observations.reason: No qualified applicants for the vacant plantilla positions; the Municipality resorted to hiring JO personnel as stop-gap measure.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC, Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) and the Bids and Awards Committee to address the causes of non-submission of Contract and POs to the Office of the Auditor within five working days after its perfection/perfection of contract, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001. Likewise, furnish the Auditor with a copy of delivery documents as required under COA Circular No. 2009-002 to facilitate the timely conduct of inspection of the deliveries. Henceforth, comply strictly COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2009-002.
observations.managementAction: Advance copies of delivery documents (Notice of Awards and Purchase Orders) together with perfected contracts are posted in PhilGEPS and copies were forwarded to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Advance copies of perfected contracts were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Inventory Committee (IC) to complete the reconciliation of Report on the
Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and PPE account balance to identify the
cause of the variance, necessary for the OIC-Municipal Accountant to effect
adjustment to accounts;

b. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to maintain updated
Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) and Property Card (PC),
respectively, necessary for reconciliation of records; and

c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to restore to PPE account the previously
derecognized PPE item without valid basis by Journal Entry Voucher with the
following entry:

Debit: PPE P1,955,000.00
Credit: Accumulated Depreciation P1,955,000.00

It is likewise recommended that the IC utilize the result of reconciliation of reports
and records for PPE in the conduct of one-time-cleansing.

Henceforth, conduct a complete cycle of physical count of PPE items owned by
the Municipal Government of Famy in compliance with the provisions of COA
Circular 80-124 dated January 18, 1980 and Manual on Property Custodianship.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to:
(a) Draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify completed projects from
Construction in Progress to appropriate PPE account; and
(b) Provide depreciation for fair presentation of accounts in the Financial
Statements.

It is likewise recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant maintain an
adequate subsidiary ledger, preferably per project, to facilitate monitoring of
accounts and in compliance with Section 111 of Presidential Decree (PD) No.
1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to coordinate with the concerned source agencies to address the discrepancies
in the Due to NGAs accounts and make necessary adjustments to ensure that the
balance of the accounts is reconciled with the books of source agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to address the discrepancies in the
reciprocal accounts Due to LGUs and Due from LGUs and make the necessary
adjustments to ensure that the balance of both accounts agrees.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to:
(a) Review records and coordinate with the Municipal General Service Office in
order to establish the composition of the PPE items acquired under the Trust
Fund account;
(b) Draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to transfer the cost of acquired PPE items
under the Trust Fund to General Fund; and
(c) Provide depreciation expenses in accordance with IPSAS No.17.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect adjustment to accounts with the
following entry:
Dr: Accounts Payable P305,123.18
Dr: Other Receivable 63.00
Cr: Due to Officers and Employees P 22,896.12
Cr: Government Equity 282,290.06

It is likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to:
(a) Observe caution in recognizing Accounts Payable, ensuring that liabilities are
only recognized at the time goods and services are accepted or rendered and
supplier/creditor bills are received, in compliance with Item s., Section 4 of
MNGAS for LGUs and Section 98 of PD No. 1445; and
(b) Conduct periodic review and analysis of Accounts Payable in order to
prevent recurrence of the noted deficiencies.

Henceforth, strictly comply with the requirements of Sections 98 and 109 of PD
No. 1445 and Section 4 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting
System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and
effect adjustments to reflect the correct balances of Due from Other Funds and
Due to Other Funds accounts.

Henceforth, conduct regular/periodic reconciliation of Due from Other Funds and
Due to Other Funds accounts in observance with the requirement of Section
111(2) and Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-
009 dated December 1, 2015 so as to ensure that reliable financial information is
provided in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to provide subsidiary record for each trust receipts recorded under
Due to NGAs account.

Henceforth, strictly comply with the requirement of Sections 111 and 112 of
Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
(a) Its Local Finance Committee to re-evaluate the placement of time deposit
under the General Fund in the amount of P4,054,668.52 in order to establish
valid basis for the account to be maintained, otherwise, terminate the same and
utilize the fund to supplement Municipality’s operating fund requirement; and
(b) The immediate termination of time deposit sourced from Trust Fund in the
amount of P1,565,198.73, making the amount of P1,500,000.00 be available to
finance Projects, Programs and Activities (PPAs) for which the amount was
received and transferring the accumulated interest of P65,198.73 to the
unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 21 and 22 of COA Circular 92-382, as
well as, Sections 3(4) and 84(2) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
General Services Officer (MGSO) to prepare documents necessary for disposal such
as (a) properly filled-up Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property
(IIRUP), indicating therein the required information, such as appraised value, mode
of disposition, etc., (b) property ledger card duly certified by the OIC-Municipal
Accountant and submit to the Audit Team for inspection and review all the
appraised value.

It is likewise recommended that the MGSO conduct a periodic review of PPE
records so as to establish the necessity of disposal.

Henceforth, conduct the immediate disposal of unserviceable property and
instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to derecognize in the books the PPE after
disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to deny request for additional cash advance if the Disbursing
Officer (DO) has an existing unliquidated cash advance.

It is likewise recommended that the Municipal Management consider paying
collective claims of its personnel such as overtime services,
communication, health emergency allowances and financial assistance to
Municipal employees, as well as wages of personnel under Job Order thru
Automated Payroll System (APS) to ensure better control over handling of cash
and administrative efficiency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a) Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal HRMO to provide a written justification or explanation on the
excess amount cash advanced for payroll fund;
b) Instruct the concerned Offices to practice caution in the preparation of
payroll;
c) Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that the amount of cash
advance for payroll fund shall be equal to the net amount of the payroll for
the pay period; and
d) Formulate additional controls for the early detection of errors and to
prevent occurrence of excessive cash advance.

Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 4.2.1 and 4.4.2 of COA Circular No.
97-002 and Section 3.1.1.2 of COA Circular No. 96-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to stop the practice of granting cash advances to elected
officials other than their official travelling expenses and strictly comply to the
provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances, as well as, observance with sound internal
control.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to require the accountable officers to liquidate cash advances
within the allowable period prescribed by COA Circular No. 97-002,
otherwise, impose penalty which may include withholding salary.

Henceforth, comply strictly with Items 4.1.3 and 5 of COA Circular No. 97-
002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
(a) Formulate a team that will address the noted deficiencies; and
(b) Instruct the team to coordinate with the seller and take actions that are
deemed necessary in order to correct the deficiencies/flaws so as to prevent the
negative impact of the subject transaction to the Municipality, which may
include the following:
 Require the seller's representative to submit all lacking documents
provided under item 13.1 of the COA Circular No. 2012-001;
 Have the transaction register with the Registry of Deeds for possible
annotation of the subject Tax Declaration while awaiting for the
completion of documents necessary for the issuance of land title;
 Require the seller's representative to post an indemnity or property bond
secured from any Insurance Company duly accredited by the Insurance
Commission to support the 70 percent claim for the subject contract in the
amount of P11,583,600.00;
 Require the seller’s representative to submit documents that will
establish validity and authenticity of the Special Power of Attorneys
(SPAs) executed by persons claiming heirs to the properties such as
judicial settlement documents duly advertised in a newspaper of general
circulation and copy of identification cards of the grantors of SPAs
exhibiting therein their signatures; and
 Coordinate with the seller’s representative to address the inconsistencies
of information presented in the Memorandum of Agreement and SPA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
(a) Immediately remit the amount of P2,821,426.30 to the Bureau of Treasury
(BTr);
(b) Exert extra effort to identify the nature, purpose and source of fund for the
unidentified accounts totaling P4,374,991.70 Due to NGAs of the Trust
Fund. Efforts may include verification of Municipal Accounting records,
coordination with other Municipal Departments/offices, as well as,
confirmation with other government agencies. Otherwise, immediately
remit the said amount to BTr; and
(c) Furnish the source agency of liquidation reports, detailing therein
information such as date, purpose, and amount of trust receipts, as well as
necessary information on utilization and subsequent remittance to the BTr.

Henceforth, comply strictly with the requirement of Section 2 of Executive Order
No. 91, series of 2019.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to remit barangay
share from real property tax within five days after the end of each quarter in
accordance with Section 271(d) of RA 7160; and to remit provincial shares from
real property and special education taxes immediately, to enable the maximum
and timely utilization of funds by the province and barangays.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
(a) OIC-Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the
adjustment to accounts using the following entry:
Debit: Due to NGAs P401,520.06
Debit: Other Payable 162,848.42
Credit: Trust Liabilities - OBO P564,368.48
(b) Concerned Building Official shall prepare a Program of Work detailing the
manner how he would utilize the 15 percent share of his Office to be
approved by the Local Chief Executive.

Henceforth, strictly comply with the requirement of, Sections 111 and 112 of PD
1445, as well as, Section 3 of DPWH-DILG JMC No. 001, series of 2013.

We recommended that the Municipal officials provide:
(a) Justification on its procurement for reconditioned vehicle instead of a brand
new one; and
(b) Justification, duly supported with verifiable documents, to address the noted
observations by the COA TAS, necessary for re-evaluation purpose.

Henceforth, comply strictly with Item 9 of Department of Budget and Management
(DBM) Budget Circular No. 2022-1 dated February 11, 2022.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct members of the
Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to:
 Ensure that PPAs listed in the LDIP are screened, evaluated and ranked
using the Urgency Test, Resource Impact and Conflict-Compatibility-
Complementarity Test; and
 Prepare Annual Investment Plan (AIP) for the 20 percent Development
Fund out of evaluated, screened and ranked PPAs of LDIP.
It is likewise recommended that the MPDC submit justification on the noted
deficiency.

Henceforth, comply strictly with the requirements of BOM for LGUs,
prescribed under Local Budget Circular No. 112 dated June 10, 2016 and
with the provisions of JMC No. 001 Series of 2007.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
(a) Instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Office, in coordination
with Municipal Engineering Office conduct periodic review of
unimplemented PPAs so as to immediately identify projects’ suitability
and feasibility, and recommend items that needs to be re-appropriated;
and
(b) Instruct the Local Finance Committee conduct periodic review the
balances of continuing appropriations as part of the annual budget
preparation so that savings are considered to finance other priority
projects, in compliance with Paragraph 2, Section 322 of the Local
Government Code.

We recommended that the Municipal Management:
(a) Provide justification on the cancelation 11 PPAs with original
appropriations in order to augment other PPAs and to finance new
project needing funds for implementation; and
(b) Submit documents proof of submission to and review of the
Sangguniang Panlalawigan (SP) for the said transfer of appropriations
as required under Section 327 of the same RA 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

observations.managementAction:
During the exit conference, the OIC, Municipal Accountant commented that some of lacking supporting documents specified in the Notice of Suspension will submit to the Office of the Auditor.
The OIC, Municipal Accountant facilitated the submission of the subject CY 2020 Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents totaling P3,840,926.82 to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Some of the lacking supporting documents were not yet submitted; Notices of Disallowance and Suspension remain outstanding pending settlement.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer immediately the unexpended balances of the Quick Response Fund and DRRMF-Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) to the Trust Liability-DRRM of the Trust Fund Book, in compliance with the aforesaid guidelines to ensure that the fund is utilized for purposes for which it was intended.
observations.managementAction: The unutilized balance of P1,302,052.02 of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) was reported in the Statement of Financial Position under Trust Liabilities-DRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to: a. stop the utilization of LDRRMF for programs, projects and activities that are not related to the DRRM activities as provided under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-CSC JMC No. 2014-1; and b. refund from the General Fund to the Trust Fund – Trust Liability- DRRM the amount of P257,264.46 representing the inappropriate utilization of the LDRRMF to programs/activities that do not fall under the specifications provided under the law and statutes and submit to this Office the journal entry voucher and the disbursement voucher of the said refund, otherwise, such will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO sent justification letter to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: For further evaluation of the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer, OIC, Municipal Accountant to: a. refrain from paying the procurement of the program thru cash advance, instead, issue check directly payable in the name of the creditor/payee to whom payment is due; b. strictly comply with the revenue regulation on withholding of taxes for the procurement of goods and services; and c. submit liquidation report/disbursement voucher to the Auditor with complete documentary requirements to validate disbursement made, to prevent the issuance of notice of suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction:
The management explained that the procurement of various supplies related to Corona virus (Covid-19) was made through cash advance due to the urgency and emergency in nature of the situation amid the pandemic. Due to Enhanced Community Quarantine implemented in the whole country, food and PPE supplies needed in response to the pandemic were very hard to find.
The OIC, Municipal Accountant has submitted the lacking documents to the Office of the Auditor on May 5, 2021.
The OIC, Municipal Accountant has submitted liquidation report to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services Officer (GSO) and the Bids and Awards Committee other concerned officials to compile and submit approved POs for the procurement of goods and furnish copies of Delivery delivery documents to the Auditor within 24 hours after acceptance of deliveries, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-002.
observations.managementAction: Regular submission of approved POs for the procurement of goods was compiled by the management. The Designated, MGSO commented that the documents will be sent through e-mail in advance to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Posting requirement in the three conspicuous places in the locality was being implemented by the Municipality; however, the website posting was not done.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. refrain from processing cash advances to accountable officers with outstanding previous cash advance/s; b. limit the grant of cash advances to the Disbursing Officer within the allowable maximum cash accountability covered by the bond of the Accountable Officer should be authorized to apply for an increase in the coverage of her bond commensurate to their actual cash accountability; and c. ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year and to observe strict compliance with the provisions of Section 5.8 of COA Circular 97-002 regarding the timely settlement and liquidation of cash advances. For long outstanding granted cash advances in previous years’, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant identify the officials responsible for the same and require them for the immediate settlement and/or accounting of the same.
observations.managementAction:
The two accountable officers with remaining unliquidated cash advances amounting to P2,263.00 December 31, 2021 was identified by the OIC, Municipal Accountant.
The OIC, Municipal Accountant has submitted the copy of JEV to effect necessary adjustments where applicable.
observations.reason: The OIC, Municipal Accountant was not able to monitor the grant of cash advances to those officers with sufficient bond. Some outstanding advances remain unliquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee, composed of the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer, to prepare the required Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Programs/Project/Activity Implementation and post the same in the Municipality’s website pursuant to the provisions under Local Budget Circular No.125. Further, send a written notice when the reports have been posted on its website to the Department of Budget and Management, House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance, and other offices where the submission of reports is required.
observations.managementAction: The management sent to the Office of the Auditor a copy of notices to the Department of Budget and Management, House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance that the submission of reports is required. The Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Programs/Project/Activity Implementation has been posted in the three conspicuous places in the locality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended several corrective actions including: (i) prepare and submit revised Annual Investment Plan (AIP) incorporating reprogramed program, projects and activities (PPAs) to COVID-related PPAs and submit to the Office of the Auditor; (ii) ensure posting requirement of the Updated Annual Procurement Plan (APP), Notice of Award (NOA) and approved contracts or Purchase Orders (POs) in the GPPB online portal and Municipality’s website; and (iii) submit immediately all the lacking supporting documents for COVID-related disbursements amounting to P15,784,623.86 and ensure that the required documents are complete before payments made in accordance with the provisions of the GPPB Circular No. 2020-01 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-01 to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction:
The management had submitted to the Office of the Auditor a revised Annual Investment Plan (AIP) including reprogramed program, projects and activities to COVID-related PPAs of which are approved by the Sangguniang Bayan.
Posting requirement in the three conspicuous places in the locality was being implemented by the Municipality. The Municipality does not have an official website.
No action was taken by the official concerned to submit immediately all the lacking supporting documents for COVID-related disbursements amounting to P15,784,623.86; and ensure that the required documents are complete before payments made in accordance with the provisions of the GPPB Circular No. 2020-01 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-01.
observations.reason: Voluminous works, lack of personnel and previous records; Municipality has no official website.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC, Municipal Accountant to record the penalties arising account to its proper account - Tax Revenue - Fines and Penalties - Property Taxes. Further, we recommend that the OIC, Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to fairly state the amount reported under RPT/SET accounts, and make necessary reconciliations with the Municipal Treasurer, and conduct continuous verification and ongoing analysis of the said accounts.
observations.managementAction:
During the year, the OIC, Municipal Accountant recorded penalties under account Tax Revenue - Fines and Penalties - Property Taxes.
Further, the OIC, Municipal Accountant informed that continuous verification and ongoing analysis of the accounts would be undertaken. The OIC, Municipal Accountant has submitted the copy of JEV to effect necessary adjustments where applicable.
observations.reason: Ongoing verification and analysis; lack of time by OIC - Municipal Accountant due to volume of work and limited manpower amid the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to designate a personnel responsible for the preparation of complete and adequate Notes to Financial Statements and disclose all the required financial information thereon to assist the users in understanding the financial statements and provide them with additional relevant information on the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Limited manpower amid the pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to:
a. prepare Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all depository
accounts, so as to account for the difference between the per book and
per bank balances of the Cash in Bank account amounting to
P3,784,765.59;
b. draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to effect adjustments to accounts, if
any; and
c. submit to the Audit Team BRS within ten days after the end of each month
to immediately establish and address the errors, omissions and
deficiencies in the Municipality’s transactions in compliance with COA
Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of PD. No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to
conduct the inventory taking annually for all the property owned/controlled by the
Municipality, prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE), render reports thereon not later than January 31 of the
following year, and submit to the Audit Team a copy of the approved report.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to continue updating and completing their Property, Plant and
Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELCs) and Property Cards (PCs) and reconcile it
with the inventory report and property records; and
Likewise, direct the OIC-Municipal GSO to continue updating and completing their
records with subsidiary ledgers of the OIC-Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Engineer to prepare the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects and provide the same to the
OIC-Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal General Services Officer
(MGSO);
b. OIC-MGSO to prepare and submit the following:
 the Report on the Local Road Network (RLRN) in the format prescribed in
Annex A of the COA Circular No. 2015-008
 the Local Road Inventory and Road Map
 the Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) in the format prescribed
in Annex E of the Circular and
 the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System (RPCRNS)
in the format prescribed in Annex C of the Circular; and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to disclose the total road network system in the
Notes to Financial Statements in the format prescribed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to:
a. refrain from processing cash advances to accountable officers with outstanding
previous cash advance/s;
b. limit the grant of cash advances to the Disbursing Officer within the allowable
maximum cash accountability covered by the bond pursuant to COA Circular No.
97-002 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009. Otherwise, Disbursing Officers should
be authorized to apply for an increase in the coverage of her bond
commensurate to their actual cash accountability; and
c. ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year and to
observe strict compliance with the provisions of Section 5.8 of COA Circular 97-
002 regarding the timely settlement and liquidation of cash advances.
For long outstanding granted cash advances in previous years’, we recommend that
the OIC-Municipal Accountant identify the officials responsible for the grant of the
same and require them for the immediate settlement and/or accounting of the same.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct
the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a. Determine the causes of the discrepancy between the reciprocal accounts Due
from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds;
b. Make the necessary correcting entries to bring both accounts in agreement; and
c. Provide the necessary disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
OIC, Municipal Accountant to fully disclose the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in
the Notes to FS in the manner described in Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We also reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that Municipal Mayor
direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer immediately
the unexpended balances of the Quick Response Fund and DRRMF-Maintenance and
Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) to the Trust Liability-DRRM of the Trust Fund
Book, in compliance with the aforesaid guidelines to ensure that the fund is utilized for
purposes for which it was intended.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Accountant to draw Journal
Entry Voucher (JEV) to make the necessary adjustment to the account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal personnel in-
charge to facilitate the transfer of titles of the acquired lands to protect the interest of the
government; and
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
reconcile her record with the record of the General Services Office who has possession
of the Torrens Certificates of Title for fair presentation of the Land account balance in the
financial statement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned persons liable to
immediately settle the subject disallowances and suspension as compliance with the
above COA Notices of Disallowance (NDs) and Notices of Suspension (NS), and
submit to the Audit Team photocopies of the Official Receipt (ORs) issued by the
Municipal Treasurer and/or other documents as proof of settlement of the amount
disallowed/suspended.

We recommended that the officials involved in the implementation of the ESP-SAP
strictly abide by the provisions of DSWD MC No. 2020-04 and Section 8 of Joint
Memorandum Circular No 1 series 2020, Sections 4(6) and 100 of Presidential Decree
No. 1445.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MSWD Officer to submit
the aforesaid lacking supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor to support
the receipt of subsidy for validation of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor the Disbursement Vouchers and their
supporting documents to ascertain the validity and propriety of transactions and to
avoid audit suspension of transactions, which could mature into disallowance.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
immediately submit the original copies of the 50 cancelled checks to the Audit Team.
Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 59 of the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs),
Volume I on the proper handling and accounting of spoiled or cancelled checks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) Secretariat to post the updated Annual Procurement Plan, Notice of Award
(NOA) together with the approved contracts or Purchase Orders (POs) in the full
disclosure board of the Municipality’s website pursuant to the provisions provided
services as required under Section 4, Item (u) RA No. 11494 and Section 3.2 of
Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) Circular No. 01-2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee,
composed of the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Planning and Development Officer, to prepare the required Report on Fund
Utilization and Status of Programs/Project/Activity Implementation and post the
same in the Municipality’s website pursuant to the provisions under Local Budget
Circular No. 125. Further, send a written notice when the reports have been posted
on its website to the Department of Budget and Management, House of
Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, Committee on Appropriations, Senate
Committee on Finance, and other offices where the submission of reports is
required.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct
the Municipal General Services Officer (GSO), Bids and Awards Committee and
other concerned officials to address the causes of non-submission of POs to the
Office of the Auditor and henceforth, ensure compliance of the pertinent provisions
under COA Circular No. 2009-001. Further, we recommend that the Municipal General
Services Officer to furnish copies of delivery documents to the Auditor within 24
hours after acceptance of deliveries, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the MDRRMO to stop the utilization of LDRRMF for programs, projects and
activities that are not related to the DRRM activities as provided under NDRRMC-
DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-CSC JMC No. 2014-1,
described above; and
b. Refund from the General Fund to the Trust Fund – Trust Liability- DRRM the
amount of P220,65.67 representing the inappropriate utilization of the LDRRMF
to programs/activities that do not fall under the specifications provided under the
law and statutes and submit to this Office the journal entry voucher and the
disbursement voucher of the said refund, otherwise, such will be disallowed in
audit.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC, Municipal Accountant (OMA) to: a. continue updating and completing their Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELCs) and Property Cards (PCs) and reconcile it with the inventory report and property records; b. draw necessary adjusting entries to correct the PPE account’s balances for fair presentation of the financial statement; OIC, Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to: a) reconcile the physical inventory report with the PPE ledger cards of the Accounting Office; b) continue updating the Property Cards (PCs) and provide the complete and necessary information therein; and c) submit the inventory report in the prescribed format to the Auditor of Property, Plant concerned not later than January 31 of each year (RPCPPE) as required under Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The OIC, Municipal Accountant has submitted the updated PPE Ledger Cards only to the Office of the Auditor on May 5, 2020, but it was not supported of paid disbursement vouchers, cancelled checks and debit/credit memos. The OIC, Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to: a) reconcile the physical inventory report with the PPE ledger cards of the Accounting Office; Not Physical inventory of the PPEs was not undertaken due to limited manpower amid the COVID-19 pandemic. b) continue updating the Property Cards (PCs) and provide the complete and necessary information therein; No action was taken by the Municipality. c) submit the inventory report in the prescribed format to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year (RPCPPE) as required under Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I. RPCPPE for CY 2020 was not prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor due to limited time and manpower amid the pandemic.
observations.reason: Lack of time by OIC - Municipal Accountant due to volume of work amid the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC, Municipal Accountant to fully disclose the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes to FS in the manner described in Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. We also reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer immediately the unexpended balances of the Quick Response Fund (QRF) and DRRMF-Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) to the Trust Liability-DRRM of the Trust Fund Book, in compliance with the aforesaid guidelines to ensure that the fund is utilized for purposes for which it was intended.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Lack of time by OIC - Municipal Accountant due to volume of work and limited manpower amid the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all depository accounts and to submit to the Audit Team within ten days after the end of each month to immediately establish and address the errors, omissions and deficiencies in the Municipality’s transactions in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of PD. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The OIC, Municipal Accountant has submitted Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2019 on May 5, 2021, but it was not supported of paid disbursement vouchers, cancelled checks and debit/credit memos.
observations.reason: Lack of time by OIC - Municipal Accountant due to volume of work amid the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned Municipal officials and employees to immediately liquidate the outstanding cash advances amounting to P35,969.68 and see to it that all outstanding cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year and that no additional cash advances are allowed to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled in compliance with Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P4,335.43 was refunded by the two Accountable Officers, details are as follows: 1) Juliana Ramirez- P4,335.43 under OR No. 9309516 dated 4/27/21; and 2) Celeste Jamilan – P993.00 under OR No 9309523 dated 4/21/21.
observations.reason: No reason given for non – liquidation of the Accountable Officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer/Designated GSO to: a. issue and renew the AREs of all the motor vehicles owned by the Municipality in accordance with Section 42 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II; b. register the vehicles in the name of Municipality in order to establish absolute ownership and control over the vehicles; and c. monitor the registration and insurance of all the motor vehicles and see to it that all vehicles are registered and insured with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS; five MVs are repairable and unserviceable.
observations.managementAction: Only three Municipality’s motor vehicles have issued AREs, and three MVs with expired AREs were renewed. MVs either lack available records required by the LTO or are unserviceable. No action was taken by the official concerned regarding registration and insurance; five MVs are repairable and unserviceable.
observations.reason: Lack of time by Designated Municipal General Services Officer due to volume of work.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC, Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to: a) pursue the disposal of the unserviceable property; b) submit to COA Auditor a Physical Inventory of all unserviceable PPEs together with the ongoing Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP); c) submit Appraisal Report and other required documents for appropriate action; and d) upon disposal, direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop from the books of accounts the sold/condemned property.
observations.managementAction: On the process of conducting the physical Inventory of all the unserviceable PPEs. IIRUP ongoing thereby IIRUP is not yet submitted. Appraisal Report and other required documents not submitted as physical inventory and unserviceable PPEs is on-going. Upon disposal, no action was taken by the management since disposal was not yet undertaken.
observations.reason: Limited time and manpower amid the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC, Municipal Accountant to submit all the lacking supporting documents on the payment of financial assistance amounting to P215,580.00 and the payrolls for the allowances duly acknowledged/signed by the payees and other relevant documents to establish validity and propriety of claims in accordance with Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The OIC, Municipal Accountant has submitted the lacking documents in the amount of P215,580.00 to the Office of the Auditor on May 5, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning Coordinator to: a. conduct a feasibility study and consultation with the Barangay Officials for the priority development projects to be implemented for the current year and ensure that only feasible and priority projects are included in the Local Development Fund as of December 31, 2020; b. coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and other Offices involved in the implementation of projects to closely monitor the implementation of the planned priority projects (PPAs) so that the projects in the locality and general public could avail of the benefits therefrom at the earliest possible time; and c. comply with the guidelines of the Joint Memorandum Circular of the DILG and DBM relative to the maximum utilization of the 20 per cent Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for development projects so that only legitimate ones are charged against the fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning Coordinator conducted a feasibility study and consultation with the Barangay Officials for priority projects to be implemented, thus increase in the utilization rate of the Development Fund as of December 31, 2020. The Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning Coordinator have coordinated and monitored Projects, Programs and Activities reprogrammed to COVID related response to the pandemic.
observations.reason: Limited implementation of priority projects due to COVID-19 restrictions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO together with the OIC, Municipal Accountant, to prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) has submitted a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the Office of the Auditor on May 5, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the original copy of the RCI and the 59 cancelled checks to the Office of the Auditor for audit purposes and ensure that all cancelled checks and supporting documents are attached to the RCI upon submission to the OIC, Municipal Accountant in accordance with the requirements of the MNGAS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has submitted the Report of Checks Issued for CYs 2019 and 2020 as she is still looking for the 59 cancelled checks.
observations.reason: Limited time and manpower amid the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services Officer (GSO), Bids and Awards Committee and other concerned officials to address the causes of non-submission of POs to the Office of the Auditor and henceforth, ensure compliance of the pertinent provisions under COA Circular No. 2009-001. Further, we recommend that the Municipal General Services Officer to furnish copies of delivery documents to the Auditor within 24 hours after acceptance of deliveries, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-002.
observations.managementAction: The management has submitted copies of POs/Contracts only to the Office of the Auditor. No action was taken by the official concerned to furnish the copies of delivery documents to the Auditor within 24 hours after acceptance of deliveries.
observations.reason: No reason given by the official concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC, Municipal Gender and Development Focal Person to submit a copy of the Annual Gender and Development Plan and Budget within five working days from the receipt of the approved plan from the PCW or their mother or central offices as the case maybe and the corresponding Accomplishment Report within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year to the COA Audit Team for the timely audit of the fund in compliance with COA Circular 2014-001.
observations.managementAction: The management has submitted a copy of the Annual Gender and Development Plan and Budget and the corresponding Accomplishment Report to the Office of the Auditor on May 5, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local GAD Focal Point Team (LGFPT) to ensure that PPAs in the GAD Plan duly address the gender issues in the locality and to establish a concrete plan specifying the programs, projects and activities to be carried out in compliance with Item 4.1 C.4 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality appropriated the amount of P4,160,769.25 for GAD related PPAs. However, due to the COVID-19 outbreak, the management has reprogrammed their GAD funds totaling P3,128,769.25 or 75.20 per cent to COVID-19 related expenses to address the needs of the people.
observations.reason: Implementation of the GAD PPAs as planned was not implemented due to the COVID-19 outbreak.

We also recommended the submission of the proof of remittance of tax withheld from this transaction.
observations.managementAction: The Disbursement Voucher paid by Check No. 266668 and its supporting documents, was submitted to the Audit Team. We also recommended the submission of the proof of remittance of tax withheld from this transaction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to submit appropriate project proposals to utilize fully all fund transfers from the National government in a timely manner for the benefit of its constituents. Otherwise, we recommended the return of the unutilized amount to the source agency so it can be distributed to other agencies with urgent need of financial assistance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to monitor the implementation of PPAs for the 20% Development Fund to help achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise all concerned officials to refund the amount of P14,200.00 as these were paid for personal and individual membership in a private organization, and to strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 7169 and NBC No. 563.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) stop paying honoraria to ALS trainers and other expenses not included in the list of priority expenses chargeable against SEF and that the LSB not use the SEF for expenditures related to the implementation of the Campus Journalism Act as those can be charged to the appropriations for DepEd.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require GAD Focal Point and other Municipal officials to ensure that programs/projects/activities directly responsive to the promotion of women empowerment and gender equality are given priority in the implementation of GAD. We also recommended that disbursements pertaining to non-GAD PPAs be charged to other appropriate accounts while ensuring GAD funds are used for GAD activities.
